title: rinorea ilicifolia subsp. spinosa
intro: Rinorea ilicifolia subsp. spinosa, also known as the thorny-leaved Rinorea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The thorny-leaved Rinorea has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Rinorea ilicifolia subsp. spinosa are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or by mixing the plant material with other herbs to create a medicinal concoction.
